title: ranunculus aquatilis var. trichophyllus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. trichophyllus, also known as the white water crowfoot, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the buttercup family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin irritations and inflammation. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis var. trichophyllus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe wounds and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or tea, although it's essential to note that its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
